INSERT INTO (SELECT currval('my_sequence_name') AS seq_val FROM my_table) VALUES (seq_val);
CREATE TABLE my_table (id INTEGER, sequence_value INTEGER);
INSERT INTO my_table (id, sequence_value) VALUES (1, nextval('my_sequence_name'));
INSERT INTO my_table (id, sequence_value) VALUES (2, currval('my_sequence_name'));
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INTEGER, seq_val INTEGER);
INSERT INTO another_table (id, seq_val) SELECT id, currval('my_sequence_name') FROM my_table WHERE id = 1;
WITH vals AS (SELECT currval('my_sequence_name') AS current_value) SELECT * FROM vals WHERE current_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT a.id, currval('my_sequence_name') FROM my_table AS a JOIN another_table AS b ON a.id = b.id;
SELECT currval('my_other_sequence') AS current_value_other_sequence, currval('my_sequence_name') AS current_value_main_sequence;
SELECT currval('my_sequence_name') + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM my_table) AS adjusted_value;
CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, current_seq INTEGER);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, current_seq) VALUES (1, currval('my_sequence_name'));
SELECT currval('my_sequence_name') AS curr_val, COUNT(*) FROM my_table GROUP BY curr_val;
